"Most retail traders make a fatal mistake: they spend hours staring at individual stock charts, completely blind to the macro forces moving them. This dashboard is built to help you strip away the noise and look directly at Wall Street's playbook."
Phase 2 · The Swimming Pool Concept
Liquidity Doesn't Vanish —
It Migrates

"Think of stock market liquidity like a massive swimming pool. Total money rarely evaporates — it sloshes from one industry to another. ETF ratios filter company-specific noise, revealing pure macro capital migration."
Phase 3 · Path of Least Resistance
Follow the Structural Higher Lows
XLK ÷ SPY Ratio — 9 Month View
XLK/SPY Ratio
● Higher Lows = Quiet Accumulation
"When a specific sector's ETF ratio is consistently making higher lows against the broader market, it sends a clear signal: institutions are quietly accumulating shares on every single dip — long before it reflects in mainstream news headlines."
Phase 4 · Anticipating Macro Squeezes
The "Glass Ceiling" Breakout
Structural resistance held 4× — then the regime shifted
Resistance Level
● 3 Rejections = Compression
● Breakout = Regime Shift
"When the ratio finally breaks above the glass ceiling, it signals a fundamental regime shift. Short sellers get trapped, institutions are forced to chase momentum — creating a high-probability environment to go long on the strongest stocks within that ETF bucket."
